titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Benjamin Trumbull Papers
titlestmt.author: Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 16090339.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday April 21, 2022 at 03:06

did.unittitle: Benjamin Trumbull Papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Letters, manuscripts, and sermons by or relating to historian and Congregational minister Benjamin Trumbull, 1757-1819. Materials document Trumbull's time at the Congregational Church in North Haven, Connecticut, and literary work on the history of Connecticut.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Correspondence, 1759-1819. II. Papers, 1757-1818. III. Sermons, 1759-1819.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Benjamin Trumbull (1735-1820) (Yale 1759) began work as pastor for the Congregational Church of North Haven, Connecticut in 1760. He served there for sixty years, interrupted only by service as a chaplain during the Revolutionary War. He wrote thousands of sermons and several historical texts, treaties, and pamphlets.

Trumbull was married to Martha Phelps Trumbull (1732-1825), with whom he had five children: David Trumbull (1762-1762), Mary Trumbull Eastman (1765-1841), Benjamin Trumbull (1769-1850), Sarah Trumbull Tuttle (1772-1817), and Elizabeth Trumbull (1774-1775).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Benjamin Trumbull Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated material: Benjamin Trumbull Papers (MS 505), Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters, manuscripts, and sermons by or relating to historian and Congregational minister Benjamin Trumbull, 1757-1819. Materials document Trumbull's time at the Congregational Church in North Haven, Connecticut, and literary work on the history of Connecticut.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Benjamin Trumbull Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Correspondence

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged alphabetically by correspondent.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seires I contains letters addressed to Benjamin Trumbull.

did.unittitle: Althearn, James - autograph letter, signed, to Zebulon Clye, forwarded to Benjamin Trumbull

Unidentified Correspondents
Papers

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is arranged roughly by format.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains drafts of letters, financial papers, and miscellaneous manuscripts of Benjamin Trumbull. Some materials pertain to Trumbull's A complete history of Connecticut, civil and ecclesiastical, from the emigration of its first planters from England, in MDCXXX, to MDCCXIII (Hartford, [Conn.] : Hudson & Goodwin, 1797). Included is a manuscript of (and subscription form for) Trumbull's Twelve discourses : comprising a systematical demonstration of the divine origin of the Holy Scriptures of the Old and New Testaments : designed for the benefit of Christians of all denominations: and especially for the instruction and edification of young people ; to establish them in a rational and full persuasion of the truth and importance of the Scriptures ; to excite their early and earnest pursuit of divine knowledge, and awaken their highest solicitude to walk with the wise, and fly the way of evil men (Harftord, [Conn.] : Hudson & Goodwin, 1799).

did.unittitle: Copies/Drafts of letters
Financial papers
Historical manuscripts and notes
Miscellaneous historical manuscripts
Miscellaneous manuscripts
Manuscript on marriage and divorce
Manuscript account of the sale of the Western Reserve
Inkhorn used while chaplain during the American Revolution
Photostatic copy (negative) of "Salutory Oration" by Jonathan Trumbull
Papers concerning Benjamin Trumbull's History of Connecticut
Miscellaneous memoranda and letters
Memoirs of Deacon Stephen Goodyear
Psalm Tunes, original and selected
Commonplace book
Twelve discourses concerning a systematical demonstration of the divine origin of the Holy Scriptures
Proposals for printing by subscription for Twelve discourses concerning a systematical demonstration of the divine origin of the Holy Scriptures
Miscellaneous addresses
Miscellaneous manuscripts
Loose notes
Missionary tour journal
Sermons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III contains circa 125 sermons delivered by Benjamin Trumbull primarily to the Congregational Church of North Haven, Connecticut.
